108.status: Has been swinging the bat and throwing without any issues and caught Yusei Kikuchi's bullpen session on July 5. Progressed to running the bases on July 10 and is nearing a rehab assignment.

Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Began a rehab assignment on July 7 in the Arizona Complex League and threw a scoreless inning with three strikeouts on July 11.
Has been hitting on the field, taking grounders and running with no issues. Started a throwing program on June 24 and is nearing a rehab assignment.

Started his throwing program and has progressed to playing catch with a targeted return date shortly after the All-Star break. He threw 20 fastballs and a mix of roughly six curveballs and changeups in his first bullpen session on July 5.
Underwent CT scan on June 10 that revealed the fracture and underwent surgery with a timeline of roughly four weeks. He's progressed to catching at the club's complex in Arizona.

Participated in light on-field activity in early June but ultimately decided to undergo right knee surgery. Can't return until July 21 at the earliest, and his surgery is expected to keep him out roughly four to six weeks.

Said on June 7 his elbow had been bothering him his last three starts when throwing changeups. MRI exam on June 8 revealed a torn ulnar collateral ligament. Underwent successful season-ending Tommy John surgery.
Sustained injury playing with Double-A Rocket City on May 31. Added to 40-man roster because he had an opt-out in his Minor League contract and placed on IL.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Felt discomfort warming up in bullpen on July 1. Underwent MRI, which revealed no ligament damage and inflammation around UCL. Will stay with team while rehabbing injury.
Underwent MRI on June 30, which revealed injury to left UCL. Scheduled to meet with Dr. Keith Meister in Arlington, Texas, on July 14.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continued to discuss treatment options. Scheduled to be evaluated by Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles in late July.
Received facet injection to lower back on June 12. Then underwent minor procedure to have bone spur removed shortly after. Set to be re-evaluated within couple of weeks, as of June 27.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Optimistic he'll be ready for start of Spring Training 2027. Nearing a running progression as of June 23.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3. Progressing to his first bullpen session on June 23.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Running in Sarasota as of June 13.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.

111.status: Second opinion on July 9 with Dr. Keith Meister in Arlington revealed the same findings as the MRI conducted by the Red Sox -- the lefty has posterior elbow inflammation.
Canceled scheduled bullpen session on July 9 and was placed on the IL. Hopeful to miss the minimum 15 days and return in first homestand after All-Star break against Orioles.
Shut down from swinging a bat due to a recent wrist hiccup. He will be checked before restarting progression.
Throwing plyo balls and working out at team's Spring Training facility in Fort Myers, Fla.

111.expectedReturn: Some time after the All-Star break
111.status: Participating in all baseball activities, though not quite at full speed. General timetable for recovery is eight to 12 weeks, and Story thinks he is closer to eight.
After grinding through injury for two months, was shut down by team's medical staff for a time period TBD.
CT scan showed a stress reaction in left forearm.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain

111.status: Was re-evaluated after some light throwing, and manager Chad Tracy cited improving numbers, saying he is “improving but not quite ready to throw yet.”
Started a throwing program in mid-June, but isn't close to a return.

112.status: Logged 4 2/3 scoreless innings (53 pitches) in rehab outing for Triple-A Iowa on July 11. Will throw during All-Star break and expectation is that he should return after that barring a setback.
Injured June 23 stretching to receive a throw at first base. Has resumed throwing, plus light running and agility work. Will throw during All-Star break with a bullpen session to follow a week later.
Resumed a throwing program (plyo ball work) on June 29. Will head to Arizona to continue rehab with a goal of throwing off the mound after All-Star break.
Diagnosed with stress reaction in bone within neck. Has started to increase activity to tolerance, including plyo ball work. Will start throwing program after All-Star break if remains asymptomatic.

Resumed throwing June 22, but unlikely to return as a starter this season. Met with Dr. Keith Meister on July 7, gaining clearance to keep building. Goal is to get off a mound the first week of August.
Logged 1 2/3 innings with three strikeouts for Triple-A Iowa on July 8. Threw 13 of his 15 pitches for strikes. Manager Craig Counsell said Maton will likely have at least one more rehab appearance.
Threw off a mound in a bullpen session on July 8, per manager Craig Counsell. Will likely have another bullpen workout before the All-Star break.
Has continued to build up in a throwing program in Arizona. Manager Craig Counsell said the lefty's timetable potentially points to an August return.
Has resumed a throwing program in Arizona. Harvey's timetable for return remains unclear, given the previous starts and stops within his comeback bid to date.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Has resumed a throwing program in Arizona. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 15 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.
Appendix flared up overnight and Milner required an appendectomy on June 27, per manager Craig Counsell. Estimated timetable is 4-6 weeks.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.

114.status: Underwent right UCL repair surgery on July 6 with Dr. Keith Meister. Typical return-to-play timeline is 10 to 12 months.
Took a line drive off his elbow on July 8. Not expected to miss significant time. Last updated: July 9)
Took batting practice for first time on July 4. Has begun to do ground-based conditioning. Team hopeful for return after All-Star break.
Has begun to hit off a tee and will progress to overhand batting practice in coming days. Underwent surgery on June 16. Remains on initial return timeline of five to seven weeks.

115.status: Hopes to soon throw a bullpen session; feels he'll be back in the rotation after facing hitters and doing rehab after the All-Star break.

115.injury: Left oblique contusion, left groin tightness
115.status: Has been shut down to allow for the healing of the groin injury that occurred during his rehab assignment (reclassified as a strain, rather than tightness).
Manager Warren Schaeffer said Hill is progressing toward a return after the All-Star break.
Has begun playing catch -- a sign of progress. The plan is for him to return after the All-Star break.
Not with the team in Los Angeles, but will join the team in San Francisco and face hitters at Oracle Park on July 10, per manager Warren Schaeffer.
Said the discomfort in shoulder was affecting his "feel" as well as his velocity.
Underwent an internal brace and repair operation on July 1.
Had surgery June 22 to have an internal brace put in his elbow. Expected recovery is one year.More >

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Underwent an MRI June 16 as doctors try to get a handle on what has stopped his rehab from advancing.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 17 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Diagnosed with stress fracture of the olecranon, a bone at the tip of the elbow, after reporting soreness June 27. Will not require surgery but will need to rest. Expected to pitch again this season.
Threw a bullpen session on July 9. Will play catch while at the All-Star Game in Philadelphia.
Threw a bullpen session on July 10. Will continue throwing at the Tigers' Spring Training facility in Lakeland, Fla., during the All-Star break.
Threw a bullpen session on July 10. Will continue throwing at the Tigers' Spring Training facility in Lakeland, Fla., during the All-Star break.
Completing a running, defense and hitting progression. Taking batting practice in cage. Could begin rehab assignment after All-Star break.

Threw 1 1/3 innings, allowing a run on two hits with a strikeout, in a rehab start with Single-A Lakeland on July 7. Velocity topped out at 100.2 mph. Will get a Spring Training-like buildup in rehab before returning.
Rejoined team and started a hitting progression on July 7. Struck by therapy band below left eye during postgame arm-care routine June 16.
Began a throwing progression on June 15.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9. Fracture is healing slowly, according to A.J. Hinch.
Underwent a hydrodissection procedure on June 10 to address lingering soreness in elbow.

117.status: Had been optioned to Triple-A Sugar Land on July 7, but that option was nullified when he was placed on IL. Injury follows a start in which he allowed 10 runs (seven earned) to the Nationals.
Banged his left knee into the wall trying to make a catch in the first inning of the July 12 game in Arlington against the Rangers and was removed for a pinch-hitter. The injury isn't serious.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Threw 4 1/3 scoreless innings (68 pitches, 44 strikes) for Triple-A Sugar Land in a Minor League rehab outing.
Threw eight pitches in one inning on a Minor League rehab outing July 11 at Triple-A Sugar Land and will pitch again July 13 for FCL Astros.
Will make second Minor League rehab outing in relief July 13 for the FCL Astros.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Threw 4 2/3 innings, allowing one run and five hits with seven strikeouts and no walks (61 pitches), in his second Minor League rehab start July 11 for Triple-A Sugar Land.
Will make fourth Minor League rehab start, this one for Double-A Corpus Christi, on July 17.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. Will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Most recent setback occurred on July 6 with continued soreness after a live BP. Had another scan done July 11 and was awaiting results to determine next steps. Transferred from 15-day to 60-day IL on July 14.

Was expected to begin rehab assignment July 7, but continuing soreness paused that plan. Got back to baseball activity on July 7, so if he progresses well, rehab assignment could come after the All-Star break.
Scratched from rehab start July 1 with shoulder soreness. Received cortisone injection July 2 and will be shut down until the All-Star break before beginning ramp-up again.
Exited fifth inning of June 30 game after experiencing discomfort in right lat area. Will continue rehab in Arizona.
Threw bullpen June 27 but suffered a setback. Received injection July 1 and will be reevaluated likely during All-Star break.
Has been battling the left hand injury for over a month and needs extended rest to let it heal. Rehabbing in Kansas City but not swinging yet. Will be reevaluated during All-Star break.
Underwent a UCL repair on left elbow July 1 and facing a 10-12 month return to play timeline.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Has started some baseball activities, but he's still not particularly close to a return to play.
Began a rehab assignment with Single-A Ontario on July 11. May not need a long stint in the Minors because he is building up for a one-inning role.
Threw his first live batting practice since having surgery on July 11. Expected to begin a rehab assignment the weekend after the All-Star break.
Has started a bullpen progression. He's currently only throwing fastballs.

Scratched from July 10 pitching start due to swelling in left knee, but will DH throughout weekend series vs. D-backs. Will have knee drained July 12 and sit out All-Star Game. Manager Dave Roberts said Ohtani's second-half pitching should not be affected.
Has resumed playing catch. He is not expected to return until after the All-Star break.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but he has had trouble ramping up.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back surgery
119.status: Underwent a surgical procedure on his back, he shared on social media.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: Manager Blake Butera said Lord “needed treatment” on July 6 after his outing on July 5. Butera thinks it will be a minimum IL stay.
Threw live batting practice on July 8 and will pitch in West Palm Beach over the All-Star break.
Allowed one run on two hits and one walk with three strikeouts over 2 1/3 innings in a rehab appearance at Triple-A Rochester on July 8.
Threw 2 2/3 scoreless innings in a rehab start at Triple-A Rochester on July 8, allowing two hits with two strikeouts.
Progressing with throwing activity as tolerated; threw out to 60 feet on July 3.
Progressing with throwing activity as tolerated from the Nationals' training complex.
Progressing with throwing activity as tolerated, had been shut down after an MRI revealed a left flexor strain following his June 16 rehab start.
Parker most likely will undergo Tommy John surgery. He exited in the bottom of the seventh inning on June 28, after issuing a four-pitch walk in which he appeared in noticeable discomfort.
Threw off the mound for the first time last week, on schedule with throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
On schedule with continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL.

121.status: Threw live batting practice for the first time on July 10, firing 40 pitches without issue, and pitched again against Minor Leaguers with Brooklyn over the All-Star break. Is nearing rehab assignment.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment on June 30 and will continue it at least through July 17. Could be activated shortly after playing for Triple-A Syracuse that night.

Began a Minor League rehab assignment on June 25 and advanced to Triple-A Syracuse on July 8. Could apply for an extension of his assignment beyond the 30-day limit, which is often granted for pitchers recovering from Tommy John surgery.
Struck on the right hand by a 92.2 mph Michael Wacha sinker on July 9. Will not require surgery but is expected to miss six to eight weeks.
Felt discomfort during a July 7 appearance against the Royals and went for an MRI the next day, which revealed no ligament damage. Will rest for around five days before restarting a throwing program.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Right thumb will remain immobilized through the All-Star break. A's will have a better idea of a timeline once the team returns from the break on July 17.
An MRI on July 10 showed no damage to the tendon in his knee. Will be immobilized in a brace for about a week from the date of injury before starting up baseball activities again.
Set to undergo season-ending knee surgery at Stanford Medical Center during the All-Star break.
Started to play catch and a swinging progression during the week of June 22. Will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
MRI on June 1 revealed a strain of the shoulder capsule and subscapularis muscle. Received a PRP injection on June 4 and will be re-evaluated 4-6 weeks from that date.
Began a throwing progression on June 11. No timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Threw a scoreless inning with Double-A Altoona on June 27. Threw 20 pitches in BP session July 3. Threw two scoreless innings in second rehab assignment July 7 for Altoona. Will pitch for Triple-A Indianapolis on July 12.

134.injury: Torn left ring finger sagittal band
134.status: Injured the finger on a diving play in Pittsburgh's game on July 5. After imaging, the injury is to the left hand ring finger sagittal band, and will sideline Griffin until at least September. He'll have a splint for six weeks.

Injured the left glute during series in Washington over Fourth of July weekend. Exited the game for a pinch-runner. Was sidelined on July 7 as a precaution. Continued to act up and leading to IL placement.

134.injury: Left hand fourth and fifth metacarpal non-displaced fractures
134.status: Cleared to begin gripping activities on June 22. No longer using cast for left hand. Began swinging in Bradenton, Fla., on July 7.

Sisk began experiencing some discomfort during the club's road trip through Philadelphia and D.C. Manager Don Kelly said the Pirates expect to learn more about the extent of the injury when they return home. Will need rest, no surgery.

Removed from game June 24 with hamstring discomfort after running the bases on a double-play ball in the third inning. Diagnosed with strain after ultrasound and MRI. Began running progression in Bradenton, Fla., on July 7.

Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day on June 7 to make room for infielder Davis Wendzel on the 40-man roster. Began side progression the first week of June. Expected to throw live batting practice first week of July.

Saw local orthopedics in Indianapolis. Information was sent to Dr. Keith Meiser, who performed his Tommy John surgery in 2022. Ligament is not an issue, per athletic trainer Todd Tomczyk. Will not throw for five to seven days.

135.status: Sidelined after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Began playing catch in late May, and began throwing bullpens in late June.

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Began playing catch again in May and began throwing bullpens in early July.
Took a hard foul ball off the mask on July 2, after also missing time in June while on the concussion IL. Cleared concussion protocol this time. Began a rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso on July 8.
Sent for further testing to determine the grade of the strain, though Taylor is hopeful it isn't serious. Injury occurred on a swing during the Padres' July 8 game vs. Arizona.
Began experiencing knee soreness in late May and landed on the IL when it worsened. Has resumed throwing bullpens -- albeit with no plan for a rehab stint yet.
Placed on the IL after pitching through left knee soreness in multiple outings. Began throwing progression week of July 6.
Struck by a comebacker. Discharged from hospital after fainting while on his way to an X-ray. Passed all tests related to fainting incident. X-rays on ankle were also negative.
MRI revealed no major structural damage, merely a strain in the subscapularis muscle. Adam expressed hope to return in early-to-mid August.
Experienced elbow soreness after his outing on June 21. Resumed light throwing in late June.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn labrum in his right hip on June 5. First sustained the injury in 2021, but it recurred and worsened over the season's first two months. Likely to miss the remainder of the season.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Exited in the second inning vs. Rays on July 12 after reaching out to grab 107.2 mph comebacker in the first inning. X-rays were negative.

Has been ramping up his baseball-related activities in Seattle. Manager Dan Wilson said on July 12 that "all signs are pointing to" Rodríguez returning to action for the first game out of the All-Star break.
Expected to begin a Minor League rehab assignment during the All-Star break.
Has moved rehab to club's Spring Training facility in Arizona. Current injury is a more serious strain in a different part of his lat than his injury earlier this season.
Slated for followup MRI exam on July 6. Injury "kind of came out of nowhere" ahead of June 9 game, per GM Justin Hollander. Will undergo another MRI in three to four weeks.
Has begun a throwing program. On a similar timeline for return to Matt Brash and Cooper Criswell.
Pulled off rehab assignment due to pain in his finger. Meeting with doctor, and if injury isn't making progress, he'll need season-ending surgery.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Currently on a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento. On track to return during the Giants' series at Seattle from July 17-19.
Continuing to rehab and will remain shut down from baseball activities through the weekend of July 10-12.
Exited July 9 in the sixth inning due to an "oblique strain of some sort." A diving catch in the first inning and swing in the fifth aggravated the issue, per manager Tony Vitello.
Currently rehab only and will be re-evaluated after the All-Star break.
Will receive a PRP injection on July 11 and be re-evaluated in four weeks.
Continuing all baseball activities, including his baserunning progression.
Suffered a setback with hamstring tightness and will be limited to throwing off flat ground through the weekend of July 10-12.
Has resumed throwing four times per week out to 75 feet.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Will go for a second opinion on a right elbow issue. Transfer to 60-day IL implies a lengthy absence is anticipated.
Had a setback during his rehab and is now dealing with a left elbow issue similar to the right elbow injury that sent him to the IL

139.status: Began a rehab stint with Triple-A Durham on July 9, allowing a run on three hits with a strikeout while recording two outs on 25 pitches. Started for Durham on July 12 and pitched 1 1/3 innings, giving up two runs on three hits and a walk.

Had rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Durham on June 30 and played three more games, but was pulled off due to continued discomfort in his groin area. He is set to visit a specialist soon, per manager Kevin Cash.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury. Had rehab assignment stalled by sprained ankle April 11, then shut down May 12 due to left shoulder inflammation. Started rehab assignment with Triple-A Durham on June 30 and most recently played second base on July 12.

Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Pitched a scoreless inning with one hit and a strikeout for the Rookie-level FCL Rays on July 10.
Felt soreness while pitching on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation. Threw 29 pitches over three innings in a rehab start for the Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays on July 7.
Had a PRP injection after a setback while playing catch. Still not throwing. Will visit a doctor soon. "The rehab has not gone as well as we would have hoped," manager Kevin Cash said.

Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, 2025. Faced hitters in live batting practice for the first time on June 30. Will soon join Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays to begin a rehab assignment.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he suffered while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 8.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

140.status: Is making his third trip to the injured list with the same injury this season.

Will not make final start before the All-Star break due to a nagging glute injury that limited him to just five innings during July 7 start. Decision will be made on a potential injured list stint in the coming days following further testing.
Expected to make first rehab outing with Double-A Frisco on July 12 ahead of the All-Star break.

Montgomery looked sharp on July 7, scattering five hits and recording three strikeouts without issuing a walk for Triple-A Round Rock, throwing 40 pitches (26 strikes) and averaging 90.7 mph with his sinker.
Received injection for hip impingement that flared up over the past week and will hopefully only be down for the 15 days.
Elbow flared up during rehab assignment and elected to have surgery. Will miss the remainder of the 2026 season.
Underwent surgery to repair a herniated disk in his neck on June 29. Expected to begin baseball activities in about three weeks.
Pulled from lineup before first at-bat on June 30, and added to the injured list on July 1.
Attempted to pitch through ankle injury before aggravating it when slipping on an on-deck circle making a play. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 23 to remove loose body.
Suffered multiple fractures on the pinky finger of his right hand after being hit by a pitch in June 13 game in Boston. Will receive additional X-rays/scans when the team returns home.
Felt something in his arm after a June 1 start in St. Louis. Scans showed elbow inflammation and he will continue to be monitored.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.

141.status: Will make another rehab start July 14 in Florida at the Blue Jays' complex and could be ready to return following the All-Star break.
Remains at the club's complex in Florida and will be reevaluated after the All-Star Break.
Will get back on the mound later this week for a bullpen and begin to build up again.
Running and hitting at the club's Florida complex as he nears a rehab assignment.
Made first rehab appearance on June 16, but was scratched from second planned rehab appearance with a neck issue, per John Schneider.
Will begin hitting over the next week or two in hopes he can be a factor in late 2026.
Will be having a scope surgical procedure on his knee after receiving a second medical opinion. It is not believed to be season-ending.
Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Will have rehab assignment moved to FCL Twins over All-Star break to continue build-up.
Underwent surgery on July 1; club is encouraged by how it went. Timetable to come after followup evaluation.
Had been managing blister condition for weeks but aggravated it in July 8 start and will miss time to recover
Underwent surgery to repair injured lat and will not pitch again in 2026
Felt soreness on July 5 after pitching July 4, which did not dissipate sufficiently following July 6 off-day. (last updated: July 8)
Suffered recurrence of lingering hip injury on July 5, but club hopes his IL stint is not a long one.
Experienced recurrence of symptoms from previous injury, but is not expected to miss extended time.
Is throwing from 105 feet and has begun hitting off a tee and in soft toss.
Threw bullpen session June 23 but still faces “a multi-week progression” before facing hitters.
Has thrown four bullpen sessions, could begin facing hitters the week of June 28.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.

143.status: Will get a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister before deciding on potential surgery. Interim manager Don Mattingly said he expects Keller to miss the rest of the season.
Landed on the IL after allowing five runs over 1 1/3 innings in his first two appearances after being recalled from Triple-A Lehigh Valley on July 4. He has a 7.14 ERA this season.
The Phillies placed Trivino on the IL before their July 8 game in Cincinnati. There is no immediate timetable for his return.

Reinstated from the restricted list on June 25 and placed on the 60-day IL. Experienced right elbow discomfort while ramping up baseball activities for return from 80-game suspension. Underwent season-ending surgery June 19.
Injured on June 10 after attempting to throw out a runner at the plate. Underwent season-ending surgery on June 24. A free agent at season's end, García is expected to be fully ready for 2027 season.

143.injury: Discogenic pain and facet inflammation in back
143.status: Underwent procedure on June 5 to address recurring back issue. Phillies hope he returns to Minor League game action in six to eight weeks.

144.status: Placed on injured list after experiencing soreness following a throw home in the sixth inning on July 10. Manager Walt Weiss said Yastrzemski is expected to need "a few days" before returning.
Could begin a rehab assignment during the All-Star break.
Still hasn't resumed throwing, but there's still hope he could return before the end of July.
Left forearm was struck by a Juan Soto line drive in a July 5 loss to the Mets. X-rays were negative, but he is awaiting results of a CT scan.
Experiencing inflammation in finger that was surgically repaired in January.
Allowed one run on three hits over three innings (41 pitches) with four strikeouts in first rehab start with Single-A Augusta on June 30.
Will likely be sidelined through the end of July, according to manager Walt Weiss, and may not return until August.
Evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister on June 15 and prescribed one month of rest. If his MRI is clean when he is re-evaluated in July, will be cleared to begin throwing progressions.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. Was in New York on June 13 for a doctor's visit when Atlanta played the Mets.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. Began throwing in early May and is up to 75 feet in his throwing progression.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.

145.status: Threw a live bullpen on July 3 as he continues the long road back from Tommy John surgery and a recent appendectomy.
Has been participating in long toss after having a setback in his rehab process. His timeline is uncertain at this point.

Will start throwing again in the next 1-2 weeks after his first injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
Threw two scoreless innings with two strikeouts for High-A Winston-Salem on July 1.
Threw one scoreless inning with two strikeouts for High-A Winston-Salem on July 1.
Progressing but still feeling some concussion symptoms and is slow to ramp up baseball activities, per manager Will Venable.
Replaced on the roster by fellow southpaw Chris Murphy.
Pulled from his injury rehab assignment and sent back to Chicago for further evaluation on June 1 after continuing to experience lower-body soreness.
Felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Exited his July 11 outing after one-plus inning and 26 pitches when seen shaking his right leg a little bit. Will undergo further tests to determine severity.

Had been feeling something off and on in his arm for several weeks but got to the point where it affected him enough to say something. Underwent further testing July 12 but the results have yet to be revealed.

MRI provided a return-to-play timeline of 2-4 weeks. Felt some tightness on July 7, then good pregame July 8. Tightness came back on defense enough that he exited the game before the top of the second inning and prior to getting an at-bat.
Was going through pitching drills in the outfield and stepped awkwardly to roll his ankle on July 8.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 3 prsopect; No. 41 overall)
146.status: Hadn't played since June 26. Previously missed time due to core muscle surgery and right wrist soreness.
Had been managing the issue, which is similar to the one he had in 2025, all season. Got to the point that he needed to rest it.
Injured during warmup drills on May 31. Timeline for return to play was approximately six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 21.

146.playerName: Thomas White (Marlins' No. 1 prospect; No. 21 overall)
146.status: Estimated timeline for return was 12-16 weeks from date of injury.More >
First felt the discomfort in mid-April in Atlanta but it never affected his pitching, just the buildup. Had trouble breathing leading up to testing. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1.More >

146.playerName: Robby Snelling (Marlins' No. 2 prospect; No. 38 overall)
146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Threw live batting practice July 11 at Double-A Somerset and “everything went well,” per manager Aaron Boone. Threw 44 pitches and had three ups. Rehab start likely on July 17.
Had a “few good days of throwing,” manager Aaron Boone reported. Did not throw July 12, but will throw every day this week starting July 13 and have a bullpen at some point.

147.playerName: Carlos Lagrange (Yankees' No. 4 prospect, MLB's No. 83)
147.status: Based on initial timeline, would resume throwing program in mid-August. “It’s going to be a close call” for him to help at the big league level this year, Brian Cashman said.
Has resumed running after sustaining a new strain to the right calf while running bases in June at Yankee Stadium. Has undergone several PRP injections to speed healing.

147.injury: Stress fracture of first rib on right side
147.status: Set for reimaging during All-Star break, which could show enough healing to increase activities. Judge has been performing lower-body exercises and weight-room work.
Continuing to log bullpen sessions. Expected to begin facing hitters in July. Could return as a starter or reliever, Brian Cashman said.

147.playerName: George Lombard Jr. (Yankees' No. 1 prospect, MLB's No. 20)
147.status: Has resumed hitting and throwing after leaving June 16 game for Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre with injury. Will play Minor League games around All-Star break.
Resumed throwing weekend of June 13-14. Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

158.status: Sent for a rehab assignment July 3 to the Arizona Complex League. Moved rehab July 10 to Triple-A Nashville.
MRI scan revealed a new anterior capsule injury, the same injury that required shoulder surgery in October 2023. Dr. Keith Meister will offer a second opinion.
Will recover and rest during All-Star break. Harrison is confident injury isn't long-term issue and that he can return quickly.
Injured his hamstring rounding second base on his two-run double on July 6 and was diagnosed with a mild strain after undergoing an MRI scan the morning of July 7.
Acquired from the A's in June, made 10 Brewers appearances in various roles before going down with injury.
Currently throwing bullpens. First live batting practice is scheduled for July 16.
Recent imaging revealed the development of torn cartilage at his kneecap. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 25 that pushed his expected return from late June to late July.
Underwent Tommy John surgery on July 1 after receiving a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister.
Underwent successful but season-ending thoracic outlet decompression surgery on June 22 in Dallas. Based on a typical recovery, should be ready for the start of next season.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11.
